_fixture_meta.label: positive
_fixture_meta.note: Synthetic fixture representing a canonical Raydium AMM v4 LP drain event. Pool had 100 prior txs, $50,000 USD liquidity, 0% LP burned pre-drain. A single Burn event removes 100% of LP supply in one tx. Signal A fires at confidence >= 0.85. Signal B would not have fired (lp_burned_pct was 0 but pool meets min_prior_txs — in practice both fire in the pre-drain latent state; this fixture isolates Signal A by providing the Burn event row). Developer MUST replace with a real Raydium v4 drained token from RugCheck rugged=true corpus before Phase 3. Use RugCheck search: rugged=true, dex=raydium_amm.
_fixture_meta.replacement_instruction: Search RugCheck API for rugged=true tokens with Raydium AMM pool. Capture pool_events burn rows from Postgres for the drain transaction. Replace this file.

_d02_burn_event_row.note: Simulated pool_events row representing the drain Burn event. In production, fetched from Postgres pool_events table.
